description: Static SF-wide rules fed to every test_plan_generation prompt. Start small (20 rules), add as specific AI failure patterns surface in generation_quality_signals.

rules.rule_text: Never include 'Id' in a create step's field_values. Salesforce assigns the Id.
Formula fields (IsClosed, IsWon, IsConverted, IsDeleted) are read-only. Never set them in create or update payloads.
System audit fields (CreatedDate, CreatedById, LastModifiedDate, LastModifiedById, SystemModstamp) are auto-managed. Do not include them in any payload.
Case has no writable Name field; it uses CaseNumber (auto-assigned). Never set Name on Case; use Subject as the human identifier.
Lead.Name is a formula (FirstName + LastName). Set FirstName + LastName separately; do not include Name in create/update.
Contact.Name is a formula (FirstName + LastName). Set FirstName + LastName separately; do not include Name in create/update.
ForecastCategory is derived from StageName via the org's stage->forecast mapping. Do not set it directly; assert on it only when the stage change is explicit.
Opportunity.Name is required on create and writable. Always supply a meaningful Name; the executor will not inject one.
A Lead with IsConverted=true is read-only; no further updates succeed. After a convert step, do NOT update the original Lead.

A successful convert on $lead exposes $lead.ConvertedAccountId, $lead.ConvertedContactId, and $lead.ConvertedOpportunityId. Use these in later record_ref / field_values; do not try to capture them via state_ref on the convert step.
For foreign-key fields (AccountId, OwnerId, *__c lookups), reference prior steps with $var or $var.Id; both resolve to the created record's 18-char Id.
Date fields use ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD for Date; YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ for DateTime). Never 'MM/DD/YYYY' or locale-dependent formats.
Picklist values are the API name, not the label. Check metadata's picklist_values list; prefer canonical values like 'Closed Won' over UI labels.
Case.IsEscalated does NOT auto-reset when Status changes. Only escalation rules / manual updates flip it. Do not assume closing a case clears it.
Polymorphic fields (Task.WhoId, Task.WhatId, Event.WhoId) accept ids for multiple SObject types. Match the id to the allowed target types; do not assume a single type.
When a RecordTypeId is needed, look it up by DeveloperName via SOQL, not by the user-facing Name. DeveloperName is API-stable.
Verify steps may only assert fields that an earlier step explicitly set, or that are safe formulas (e.g. IsClosed after StageName change). Do not invent values.
Verify assertions on picklist fields compare against the API value, not the label. Use the same value your create/update step set.

For negative_validation / boundary TCs that probe a validation rule firing, set expect_fail=true on the single step you expect Salesforce to block. Without it, the framework reports the whole test as failed.
In Salesforce validation formulas, a null numeric field does NOT equal 0. Rules like 'Amount > 0' fire when Amount = 0 but NOT when Amount is null. Set fields explicitly when exercising a rule.
